```json
  "decision": "fail",

  "work_stage": "in-progress",
  "component_conditions": ["installed-defective"],
  "percent_complete": 30,
  "value": 0,

  "confidence": 0.95,

  "rate": 0,
  "order": [],
  "workable": [],
  "findings": [
    "Hot work is being performed over an open sewer/drain without documented isolation, purging, or gas-free verification.",
    "No evidence of a fire watch or standby extinguishing equipment positioned at the point of work.",
    "Combustible vapors may accumulate in the sewer/drain and be ignited by sparks or slag from hot work.",
    "Required hot-work permit has not been shown to address the specific hazard of work over an opening to a combustible atmosphere."
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Stop hot work immediately and remove ignition sources from the area.",
    "Issue a formal stop-work order and notify the site safety officer and competent person.",
    "Inspect and test the sewer/drain atmosphere for flammable gases/vapors and oxygen levels before authorizing resumption.",
    "Cover, seal, or otherwise positively isolate the sewer/drain opening to prevent sparks, slag, or heat from entering.",
    "Purge, ventilate, or inert the sewer/drain atmosphere as required by the hazard assessment.",
    "Establish a dedicated fire watch with appropriate extinguishing equipment within immediate reach.",
    "Complete a revised hot-work permit that specifically addresses work over sewers/drains and is signed by the authorizing individual.",
    "Verify all controls with the competent person before restarting."
  ],
  "rationale": "NFPA 51B and OSHA 1926 Subpart J require that hot work over or near openings, sewers, or drains that could contain combustible atmospheres be preceded by isolation, purging/inerting, atmospheric testing, and a fire watch. Performing hot work over a sewer/drain without these controls is a critical sequence violation because it creates an imminent fire/explosion hazard.",
  "references": [
    "NFPA 51B: Standard for Fire Prevention During Welding, Cutting, and Other Hot Work",
    "OSHA 1926 Subpart J - Welding and Cutting"
  ]
}
```
